app.title: AI Watermark Remover
app.description: Clean your AI-generated images in one click — strip watermarks, wipe metadata, remove labels. Free and open.

upload.dropzone: Drop an image here or click to browse

detection.title: Scan results
detection.clean: We didn't find any recognizable AI watermarks or metadata signatures in this image.

mode.title: Cleaning method
mode.standardDesc: Erases visible overlays and embedded metadata. Works in seconds on CPU.
mode.deepDesc: Pixel-level regeneration to disrupt invisible watermarks. GPU required.

result.newImage: Start over with a new image

visible.title: Visible marks
visible.desc: Pattern-matched detection locates logos, sparkles, and text badges, then content-aware inpainting fills the region seamlessly.

invisible.title: Hidden signals
invisible.desc: Pixel-domain regeneration disrupts embedded patterns like SynthID while preserving image quality at native resolution.

metadata.title: Data layers
metadata.desc: C2PA manifests, EXIF tags, PNG text chunks — all AI-origin metadata is identified and stripped from the file.

showcase.title: See it in action
showcase.subtitle: Original and cleaned side by side, with the watermark corner magnified — the mark is erased while the rest of the photo stays pixel-identical.

platforms.title: Supported sources
platforms.subtitle: Handles watermark formats across major generative AI platforms.

q1.q: How does the cleaning process work?
q1.a: We run a two-phase pipeline. First, visible overlays are detected via pattern matching and filled with surrounding pixels. Then metadata fields — C2PA, EXIF, PNG chunks — are stripped. The output is a clean file with no AI-origin labels.

q2.a: Standard Clean only touches the tiny region around the watermark. The rest of the image is pixel-identical to the original. Deep Clean regenerates at native resolution with quality preservation.

q3.q: What file types and sizes are accepted?
q3.a: PNG, JPEG, and WebP. Maximum 15 MB per file. PNG is recommended for best results with invisible watermarks.

q4.q: What happens to my uploaded images?
q4.a: Images are processed in memory where possible. Temporary files are purged within 60 minutes. Nothing is stored, shared, sold, or used for training.

q5.q: Can I process multiple images at once?
q5.a: The web interface handles one image at a time. For batch processing, try the open-source CLI tool — it works on entire directories in one command.

error.title: Something went wrong
error.invalidFormat: Unsupported format. Use PNG, JPG, or WebP.

footer.privacy: Files are auto-deleted within 60 minutes. Nothing is stored, shared, or sold.
